Authentication: Default: uses Google application default credentials from the local environment.
CredentialsFile: populate CredentialsFile with a Google credential JSON file path for generic file-based Google auth flows.
ServiceAccount: populate CredentialsFile with a service-account JSON key and share target docs or folders with that service account email.
AccessToken: populate OAuthClientSecretsFile with a Desktop app OAuth client JSON. The sample opens the browser, caches the refresh token locally, and acquires the access token on startup.
ApiKey: populate ApiKey for public Google Docs read scenarios. Create, edit, and appData-backed payload operations usually require OAuth credentials.
ServiceAccount setup steps:
1. Open https://console.cloud.google.com/ and create or select a Google Cloud project.
2. Go to APIs & Services > Library and enable Google Drive API for that project.
3. Go to IAM & Admin > Service Accounts and create a service account.
4. Open that service account, create a JSON key, and download the credential file.
5. Share the target Google Docs or folders with the service account email so it can read or edit them.
6. Set Type=ServiceAccount below and point CredentialsFile at the downloaded JSON file.
AccessToken interactive OAuth setup steps:
1. Open https://console.cloud.google.com/ and create or select a Google Cloud project.
2. Go to APIs & Services > Library and enable Google Drive API for that project.
3. Go to APIs & Services > OAuth consent screen and complete the app registration if Google prompts for it.
4. Go to APIs & Services > Credentials and choose Create Credentials > OAuth client ID.
5. Choose Desktop app, create the client, and download the OAuth client JSON file.
6. Set Type=AccessToken below and point OAuthClientSecretsFile at that JSON file.
7. Go to Audience > Publish the app or added Test users
8. Run the sample and complete the browser sign-in flow; the refresh token is cached under OAuthTokenStoreDirectory.
ApiKey setup steps:
1. Open https://console.cloud.google.com/ and create or select a Google Cloud project.
2. Go to APIs & Services > Library and enable Google Drive API for that project.
3. Go to APIs & Services > Credentials and choose Create Credentials > API key.
4. Optionally restrict the key to Google Drive API and your expected app or network usage.
5. Set Type=ApiKey below and paste the generated key into ApiKey.
6. Use ApiKey mode mainly for public Google Docs reads; write/edit flows usually need OAuth credentials instead.
